name: Checker
comment:
Implements a checker that ensures a data model is valid as a level 2
construct.


# Model checking processor.

@docs processorImpl, errorCatalogue

values.name: errorCatalogue
values.comment: The error catalogue for this checker.

values.name: processorImpl
values.comment:
SPI for this L2 checker. Use `L2.builder` to create an `L2.Processor` out
of this.

name: Errors

aliases.name: Error
aliases.comment:
Common Error definition, for reporting errors to users.

This contains all the information needed to build a nice human readable error
message. The body is written in `mdgriffith/elm-markup` and looks like this:

    There was an error in your code.

    |> Source
        label = Look, here it is:
        pos = 0

    Please fix it. This is not a helpful error message.
aliases.type: { code : Basics.Int, title : String.String, body : String.String, args : Dict.Dict String.String String.String, sources : List.List SourcePos.SourceLines }

aliases.name: ErrorBuilder
aliases.comment:
Defines the signature of a function for building Errors. This keeps the
positional information variable, but requires a function to turn source code
positions into quotes lines of source code.

Typically implementation of this will use the `lookupError` functions and an
error catalogue of error messages.

aliases.name: ErrorMessage
aliases.comment:
Defines the content of an error message.

The body is written in `mdgriffith/elm-markup` and looks like this:

    There was an error in your code.

    |> Source
        label = Look, here it is:
        pos = 0

    Please fix it. This is not a helpful error message.

An `ErrorMessage` will be combined with some lines of source code that it can
quote, in order to produce an `Error`.

values.name: asConsoleString
values.comment: Renders an Error as a String for printing to the console with ANSI colours.

values.name: document
values.comment: elm-markup Document describing the format of an error.

values.name: htmlRenderer
values.comment: Renders an Error as HTML.

values.name: lookupError
values.comment:
Looks up an ErrorMessage in an error catalogue and fills in its quoted
source code and parameters to produce an Error.
values.type: Dict.Dict Basics.Int Errors.ErrorMessage -> Basics.Int -> Dict.Dict String.String String.String -> List.List SourcePos.SourceLines -> Errors.Error

values.name: lookupErrorNoArgs
values.comment:
Looks up an ErrorMessage in an error catalogue and fills in its quoted
source code to produce an Error.
values.type: Dict.Dict Basics.Int Errors.ErrorMessage -> Basics.Int -> List.List SourcePos.SourceLines -> Errors.Error

name: L1

unions.name: Container
unions.comment: Containers for 0..n instances of some type.

unions.name: Declarable
unions.comment: Things that can be declared as named constructs.
cases: List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, List.List ( String.String, L1.Type pos ref, L1.Properties ) )

unions.name: PropSpec
unions.comment: Defines the kinds of additional property that can be placed in the model.

unions.name: Property
unions.comment:
Allows additional properties from a variety of possible kinds to be placed
in the model.

unions.name: Restricted
unions.comment: Restricted forms that are subsets of the basic data types.
cases: { min : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, max : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, width : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int }
{ minLength : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, maxLength : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, regex : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

unions.name: Type
cases: List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, L1.Type pos ref, L1.Properties )

unions.name: Unchecked
unions.comment: Indicates that the model has not been checked.

aliases.name: L1
aliases.comment: L1 is a list of unchecked declarables.

aliases.name: PropSpecs
aliases.comment:
A set of additional property kinds that can or must be defined against
the model.

aliases.name: Properties
aliases.comment: A set of additional properties on the model.

values.name: defineProperties
values.comment:
Defines a set of property specifications with possible defaults. The first argument is a
list of property specs, and the second is a list of default values.

Values may appear in the second argument that are not in the first, in which case a specification
for them will be infered.

Values in the second argument that are also in the first should be of the same kind, but will be
overriden by the second argument if not.
values.type: List.List ( String.String, L1.PropSpec ) -> List.List ( String.String, L1.Property ) -> ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties )

values.name: emptyProperties
values.comment: Creates an empty set of properties.

values.name: positionOfDeclarable
values.comment: Gets the position context from a Declarable.

values.name: positionOfType
values.comment: Gets the position context from a Type.

values.name: propertiesOfDeclarable
values.comment: Gets the properties from a Declarable.

name: L2

unions.name: RefChecked
unions.comment:
Indicates that named types in the model have been reference checked to
enusure that they name something that actually exists in the L2 dictionary.

Some summary information of what they refer to is also cached during ref
checking. This can be very convenient during code generation to avoid having
to look up in the dictionary all the time, to find out what kind of thing a
named type is.

aliases.name: L2
aliases.comment:
L2 is a dictionary of named data model components, which has also been
reference checked to ensure that all references in the model have named
entries in the dictionary.

aliases.name: Processor
aliases.comment: API for an L2 model processor.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, check : L1.L1 pos -> ResultME.ResultME Errors.Error (L2.L2 pos) }

aliases.name: ProcessorImpl
aliases.comment:
SPI for an L2 model processor. Use the `builder` to turn one of these into
a `Processor`.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, check : L1.L1 pos -> ResultME.ResultME err (L2.L2 pos), buildError : Errors.ErrorBuilder pos err }

values.name: builder
values.comment:
Builds an L2 Processor API from an implementation. A function to turn
source code positions into quoted lines of source code needs to be supplied.
values.type: (pos -> SourcePos.SourceLines) -> L2.ProcessorImpl pos err -> L2.Processor pos

name: L3

unions.name: PropCheckError
unions.comment:
Once properties have been checked, then reading properties as per the property
specification should always succeed, since those properties have been verified to be
present and of the correct kind.

If reading a property fails, it is a coding error and should be reported as a bug.
This error type enumerates the possible properties bugs.

aliases.name: DefaultProperties
aliases.comment: Allows the default properties on parts of the model to be defined.
aliases.type: { top : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), alias : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), sum : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), enum : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), restricted : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), fields : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), unit : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), basic : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), named : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), product : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), emptyProduct : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), container : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), function : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ) }

aliases.name: L3
aliases.comment:
The L3 model. This consists of an L2 model and a set of top-level properties
that a code generator needs to know.
aliases.type: { properties : L1.Properties, declarations : Dict.Dict String.String (L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked) }

aliases.name: Processor
aliases.comment: API for an L3 model processor.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, defaults : L3.DefaultProperties, check : L3.L3 pos -> ResultME.ResultME Errors.Error (L3.L3 pos) }

aliases.name: ProcessorImpl
aliases.comment:
SPI for an L3 model processor. Use the `builder` to turn one of these into
a `Processor`.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, defaults : L3.DefaultProperties, check : L3.L3 pos -> ResultME.ResultME err (L3.L3 pos), buildError : Errors.ErrorBuilder pos err }

aliases.name: PropertiesAPI
aliases.comment: An API for supplying property readers for various parts of the data model.
aliases.type: { top : L3.PropertyGet, declarable : L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked -> L3.PropertyGet, field : L1.Properties -> L3.PropertyGet, type_ : L1.Type pos L2.RefChecked -> L3.PropertyGet }

aliases.name: PropertyGet
aliases.comment: An API for reading properties of various expected kinds.
aliases.type: { getStringProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError String.String, getEnumProperty : Enum.Enum String.String -> String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError String.String, getQNameProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError (List.List String.String), getBoolProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError Basics.Bool, getOptionalStringProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError (Maybe.Maybe String.String), getOptionalEnumProperty : Enum.Enum String.String -> String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError (Maybe.Maybe String.String) }

values.name: builder
values.comment:
Builds an L3 Processor API from an implementation. A function to turn
source code positions into quoted lines of source code needs to be supplied.
values.type: (pos -> SourcePos.SourceLines) -> L3.ProcessorImpl pos err -> L3.Processor pos

values.name: errorBuilder
values.comment: Convert prop check errors to standard errors.

values.name: errorCatalogue
values.comment:
The error catalogue for this property checking.

The error message here are quite generic, and you likely want to re-write
this error catalogue for specific modules in order to give better messages.

values.name: makePropertiesAPI
values.comment:
Creates a properties API from a set of defaulted property specs, and an
L3 model.

name: Naming

values.name: checkName
values.comment:
Checks that a name starts with an alpha character, and is followed only
by alpha and numeric characters.

values.name: safeCCL
values.comment:
CCL stands for camel-case-lower. This puts a name into camel case lower
format.

Additional it checks if the name matches an Elm keyword, and proposes a
different name to use instead, which is the original with an underscore appended.

    safeCCL "type" == "type_"

    safeCCL "someVar" == "someVar"

    safeCCL "MyList" == "myList"

values.name: safeCCU
values.comment:
CCL stands for camel-case-upper. This puts a name into camel case upper
format.

Additional it checks if the name matches an Elm keyword, and proposes a
different name to use instead, which is the original with an underscore appended.

    safeCCL "type" == "Type_"

    safeCCL "someVar" == "SomeVar"

    safeCCL "MyList" == "MyList"

values.name: safeName
values.comment:
Checks if a name matches an Elm keyword, and proposes a different name to
use instead, which is the original with an underscore appended.

    safeName "type" == "type_"

values.name: sortNamed
values.comment: Sorts a list of named things alphabetically.
values.type: List.List ( String.String, a ) -> List.List ( String.String, a )

values.name: sortNonemptyNamed
values.comment: Sorts an non-empty list of named things alphabetically.
values.type: List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, a, b ) -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, a, b )

name: SourcePos
comment:
SourcePos provides a way of defining regions of text to quote or to
highlight mainly for the purpose of showing where errors exist within some
source code.

The code is treated as lines of text, and positions within that described by row
and column number.


# Regions of text

@docs Region, RowCol


# Quoted and highlighted blocks of text

@docs SourceLines, sourceLinesForRegions

aliases.name: Region
aliases.comment: Describes a region of text running from a start to an end position.

aliases.name: RowCol
aliases.comment: Describes a position within some lines of text.

aliases.name: SourceLines
aliases.comment:
Captures a set of source code lines which are indexed by their line numbers,

A highlight region may also be specified, and if set is used to indicate where
within the source lines an error is located. All lines within the highlight
region are expected to be in the line dict.
aliases.type: { lines : Dict.Dict Basics.Int String.String, highlight : Maybe.Maybe SourcePos.Region }

values.name: sourceLinesForRegions
values.comment: Quotes a region of text as `SourceLines`.
values.type: Dict.Dict Basics.Int String.String -> List.List SourcePos.Region -> List.List SourcePos.SourceLines

name: Templates.Elm
comment:
Elm code generation from L2 models. This can be used when writing an L3 code
generator that output Elm code. It can be used to generate type declarations,
type annotations and JSON codecs for Elm data models.

Type declarations:

@docs typeDecl, codec

Lowerings of L2 into Elm type annotations:

@docs lowerType, lowerFun

values.name: codec
values.comment: Generates a Codec for an L1 type declaration.
values.type: String.String -> L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked -> ( Elm.CodeGen.Declaration, Elm.CodeGen.Linkage )

values.name: lowerFun
values.comment: Lowers an L1 function type into an Elm type annotation
values.type: L1.Type pos L2.RefChecked -> L1.Type pos L2.RefChecked -> ( Elm.CodeGen.TypeAnnotation, Elm.CodeGen.Linkage )

values.name: lowerType
values.comment: Lowers an L1 type into an Elm type annotation.

values.name: typeDecl
values.comment:
Turns an L1 type declaration into Elm code.

A type can result in a list of declarations - enums in addition to declaring a
type can also declare the permitted enum values.
values.type: String.String -> Elm.CodeGen.Comment Elm.CodeGen.DocComment -> L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked -> ( List.List Elm.CodeGen.Declaration, Elm.CodeGen.Linkage )
